text: Last summer, I planned a two-week trip to Japan. I spent weeks researching places to visit and booking hotels, feeling both excited and nervous. Once there, I was amazed by the new culture – from the peaceful temples to the busy city streets. The food was a highlight; I tried sushi from a tiny shop and warm ramen on a rainy evening. However, language barriers were tricky. I couldn’t read menus well, so I often pointed at pictures and smiled. Once, I got lost, but a kind local helped me with simple English. Despite the challenges, the trip created wonderful memories. I still think about the kindness of strangers and the delicious meals, reminding me that travel is always worth the effort.
